description: Explicit role dependency declarations via meta/main.yml to replace implicit play.yml ordering

userStories.title: Bootstrap empty meta/main.yml for all 74 roles
userStories.description: As a contributor, I need every role to have a meta/main.yml with dependencies: [] and allow_duplicates: false so the dependency declaration infrastructure exists for all subsequent work.
acceptanceCriteria: All 74 roles under roles/ have a meta/main.yml file
Every meta/main.yml contains 'dependencies:' key (empty list is fine) and 'allow_duplicates: false'
The 16 roles that already have meta/main.yml are updated to include allow_duplicates: false
ansible-playbook play.yml --syntax-check passes
userStories.notes:
Bootstrap with: for dir in roles/*/; do [ ! -f "${dir}meta/main.yml" ] && printf -- "---
dependencies: []
allow_duplicates: false
" > "${dir}meta/main.yml"; done

userStories.title: Add scripts/validate_deps.py and make validate-deps target
userStories.description: As a contributor, I want a validator that walks the role dependency graph and fails on cycles or references to nonexistent roles, so mistakes are caught before ansible-playbook runs.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/validate_deps.py exists and is executable
make validate-deps target exists and is documented in make help output
make validate-deps passes on the current codebase (all deps empty)
Introducing a cycle in any meta/main.yml causes make validate-deps to exit non-zero with a CYCLE: error message
Referencing a nonexistent role in dependencies: causes make validate-deps to exit non-zero with a clear error
ansible-playbook play.yml --syntax-check passes
userStories.notes: Blocked by US-001. See PRD for reference 50-line implementation.

userStories.title: Wire validate-deps as prerequisite of make configure
userStories.description: As a contributor, I want make configure to automatically validate the dependency graph before invoking ansible-playbook, so a broken graph aborts before Ansible starts.
acceptanceCriteria: make configure Makefile target lists validate-deps as a prerequisite
make configure TAGS='...' is also gated by validate-deps
A broken dependency graph causes make configure to abort before ansible-playbook is invoked
make configure with a valid graph proceeds normally with no behavior change
ansible-playbook play.yml --syntax-check passes
userStories.notes: Blocked by US-002. Change is a one-line Makefile edit: configure: req-playbook validate-deps

userStories.title: Declare x and lightdm dependency chain
userStories.description: As a user running make configure TAGS=i3, I want the x role to run automatically before i3 so I get a working environment without having to know the dependency.
acceptanceCriteria: roles/i3/meta/main.yml declares '- role: x' in dependencies
roles/lightdm/meta/main.yml declares '- role: x' in dependencies
ansible-playbook play.yml --tags i3 --list-tasks shows x tasks before i3 tasks
make validate-deps passes
ansible-playbook play.yml --syntax-check passes
userStories.notes: Blocked by US-001. Can run in parallel with US-005 and US-006.

userStories.title: Declare gpu_detect and gpu_drivers dependency chain
userStories.description: As a user running make configure TAGS=gpu_drivers, I want gpu_detect to run first automatically so driver selection has detection results to read.
acceptanceCriteria: roles/gpu_drivers/meta/main.yml declares '- role: gpu_detect' in dependencies
ansible-playbook play.yml --tags gpu_drivers --list-tasks shows gpu_detect tasks before gpu_drivers tasks
make validate-deps passes
ansible-playbook play.yml --syntax-check passes
userStories.notes: Blocked by US-001. Can run in parallel with US-004 and US-006.

userStories.title: Declare Hyprland 7-role dependency chain
userStories.description: As a user running make configure TAGS=hyprland, I want all prerequisite roles (wayland, qt_gtk_toolkit, widgets, and their transitive deps) to run automatically in the correct order.
acceptanceCriteria: roles/hyprland/meta/main.yml declares dependencies on wayland, qt_gtk_toolkit, widgets
roles/qt_gtk_toolkit/meta/main.yml declares dependency on wayland
roles/widgets/meta/main.yml declares dependency on qt_gtk_toolkit
uv_python_packages, microtex, oneui4_icons, screencapture are reviewed and wayland dependency added where appropriate
ansible-playbook play.yml --tags hyprland --list-tasks shows wayland before qt_gtk_toolkit before widgets before hyprland
No duplicate execution of wayland when multiple dependents are included
make validate-deps passes
ansible-playbook play.yml --syntax-check passes
userStories.notes: Blocked by US-001. Can run in parallel with US-004 and US-005.

userStories.title: Audit dependencies: base system roles
userStories.description: As a contributor, I want the base system roles to have reviewed and confirmed dependency declarations so their meta/main.yml accurately reflects their real runtime prerequisites.
acceptanceCriteria: Each of the following roles has a reviewed dependencies list: base, microcode, grub, systemd, system, filesystem, sysmon, cron, gnupg, regdomain, mirrorlist
Any newly-declared dependency includes a comment in meta/main.yml explaining why
make validate-deps passes
ansible-playbook play.yml --syntax-check passes
userStories.notes: Blocked by US-001 and US-002. HITL: requires human judgment per role. Can run in parallel with US-008 through US-011.

userStories.title: Audit dependencies: utility and service roles
userStories.description: As a contributor, I want the utility and service roles to have reviewed and confirmed dependency declarations, including converting goesimage's hardcoded cross-role include_tasks to a proper dependencies declaration.
acceptanceCriteria: Each of the following roles has a reviewed dependencies list: pass, taskwarrior, spell, clipboard, clouddrive, syncthing, filesharing, cups, udisks, nmtrust, networkmanager, nettools, laptop, goesimage, notes, widgets, microtex, oneui4_icons, uv_python_packages, archive, dotfiles
goesimage no longer uses hardcoded include_tasks: roles/nmtrust/tasks/unit.yml — replaced with dependencies: [{role: nmtrust}]
Any newly-declared dependency includes a comment in meta/main.yml explaining why

ansible-playbook play.yml --syntax-check passes
